formatting_rules.followup_signature: Do not add sender name, initials, website, or full signature in follow-up copy.

examples: around 10 AM, 11 AM, or noon
late morning or early afternoon
around noon or shortly after
Tuesday morning or Wednesday afternoon

scheduling_rules.thursday_or_friday_logic: If prospect replies Thursday or Friday, propose Monday or Tuesday next week. If prospect replies Monday, propose Wednesday or Thursday.
scheduling_rules.proposal_style: Whenever proposing times, suggest 2 working days and provide 2 to 3 time windows for each day in a natural human way. Example: Monday around 10 AM, 11 AM, or noon, or Tuesday around 11 AM, noon, or early afternoon. Use natural terms like noon, afternoon, early afternoon instead of rigid timestamps whenever appropriate.

positive_share_more_info.intent: Prospect says yes, interested, send more info, share more, sounds good, or similar.
positive_share_more_info.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

The goal is to {{ultimate_business_outcome_you_help_them_achieve}}.

We do this by optimizing your inbound flow and running outbound campaigns. We work on your existing lead flow, and if there are any of your ideal prospects visiting your website or ad landing pages but not filling out a form or contacting your team, we identify those companies, route them into personalized email campaigns within minutes, and increase your booked calls. Alongside that, we also source cold lists of your ICPs and run highly targeted outbound campaigns in parallel.

The goal is not to add another SEO or ad effort to bring in more visitors, but to ensure your existing resources and marketing efforts are utilized at full capacity, driving 10–20 qualified sales conversations each month.

I’d be glad to walk you through {{3_to_4_relevant_call_agenda_items}}.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work for you? Please let me know and I will send the invite.

{{ultimate_business_outcome_you_help_the.description: The high-level business result they actually care about.
examples: build a more predictable pipeline of enterprise buyers
generate more qualified conversations with franchise operators
create consistent meetings without relying only on referrals
get in front of high-intent SaaS decision-makers

{{3_to_4_relevant_call_agenda_items}}.description: Specific things that make the call feel valuable and concrete.
examples: how the visitor identification system works
examples of outbound campaigns in their industry
what messaging angles are currently converting
realistic meeting volume expectations
deliverability and infrastructure setup
how qualification and booking are handled
Thursday at 11 AM, noon, or 1 PM
Friday at 10 AM, noon, or 1 PM

positive_with_context.intent: Prospect says yes and adds context, humor, concern, opinion, or business detail.
positive_with_context.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

{{casual_response_to_prospect_comment}}

The goal is to help {{companyName}} create a more predictable pipeline of qualified sales conversations without relying entirely on referrals, inconsistent inbound, or manual outreach.

We do this by optimizing your existing lead flow and running highly targeted outbound campaigns in parallel. If ideal prospects visit your website or landing pages without contacting your team, we identify those companies and route them into personalized outreach quickly while also sourcing and targeting cold ICP lists through outbound email campaigns.

The goal is not to add another SEO or ad effort to bring in more visitors, but to ensure your current marketing and sales efforts are utilized fully, driving 10–20 qualified sales conversations each month.

I’d be glad to walk you through {{call_agenda_3_to_4_items}}.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work for you? Please let me know and I will send the invite.

meeting_ready.intent: Prospect says let’s meet, send times, book a call, happy to chat, calendar, or similar.
meeting_ready.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Perfect. I’d be glad to walk you through how Ascendly helps companies turn existing traffic and outbound into qualified sales conversations without adding more tools or internal sales overhead.

We’ll cover how visitor identification works, how we structure personalized outbound campaigns, what kind of messaging is converting right now, and what realistic meeting flow could look like for {{companyName}}.

I am available {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}}. Let me know what works best and I will send the invitation.

pricing_question.intent: Prospect asks about pricing, quote, package, rates, budget, or cost.
pricing_question.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Pricing is a fair starting point, but let’s first figure out if, assuming there’s no pricing constraint, if our approach would actually fit {{companyName}} and support the kind of pipeline consistency you are aiming for.

What you should expect is, we structure our pricing either as a monthly management engagement or a lighter optimization/audit project first, depending on how hands-on you want us to be after the initial review.

Usually, we look at how qualified opportunities are currently coming in, where potential conversations are being lost, and whether combining visitor identification with targeted outbound could help create a more controlled flow of sales meetings.

The goal is not to add another marketing expense, but to make sure your existing traffic, campaigns, and outbound efforts are producing more qualified conversations consistently.

I’d be glad to walk you through how this works, examples from similar campaigns, and what realistic meeting volume could look like for your market.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work?

followup_1.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

{{light_contextual_reference_or_question}}

I shared a couple of times last time but never saw a confirmation come through, so I wanted to check back before the conversation goes cold.

The goal here is simply to see whether {{companyName}} could benefit from turning more existing traffic and outbound activity into qualified sales conversations consistently.

Does {{previous_proposed_time_1}} or {{previous_proposed_time_2}} still work for you? If not, happy to adjust around your schedule.

followup_2.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Still interested in learning whether {{companyName}} is currently focused on creating a more predictable pipeline of qualified sales conversations.

Ascendly helps companies maximize existing marketing and outbound efforts by identifying missed opportunities from inbound traffic while also running highly targeted outbound campaigns in parallel.

The goal is not to add another marketing layer, but to ensure current efforts are utilized fully and consistently generate qualified conversations.

Would {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work for a quick conversation?

followup_3.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Wanted to circle back because I genuinely think there may be an opportunity for {{companyName}} to generate more qualified conversations without increasing ad spend or relying entirely on referrals.

We help companies identify ideal prospects already interacting with their website or campaigns, route them into personalized outreach quickly, and combine that with targeted outbound campaigns built around your ICP.

I’d be glad to walk you through the system, campaign structure, messaging approach, and what kind of meeting flow could realistically make sense for your market.

Does {{proposed_time_1}} or {{proposed_time_2}} work?

followup_4.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

Most companies do not struggle because they lack traffic or lead sources.

Usually, the issue is that existing opportunities are never fully captured, qualified, or re-engaged consistently.

That is exactly where Ascendly fits in. We help companies turn existing inbound activity and outbound efforts into a more controlled pipeline of qualified sales conversations.

Curious if that lines up with what {{companyName}} is trying to improve right now?

Happy to connect next week if it makes sense.

followup_5.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

At the core, Ascendly helps companies create a more predictable sales pipeline by combining personalized outbound campaigns with inbound opportunity capture.

The idea is not to generate more random traffic, but to make sure fewer qualified opportunities slip through the cracks while driving more sales conversations consistently.

Should I just send over my calendar options and let you decide if a quick conversation makes sense?

followup_6.template:
Hey {{firstName}},

I’ve reached out a few times but haven’t heard back, so I’m assuming improving pipeline consistency is probably not a priority for {{companyName}} right now, is that fair?

Either way, appreciate the time and certainly do not want to be a pest.

{{light_contextual_reference_or_question.description: A short contextual opener tied to the previous conversation.
examples: enjoyed your point about referrals
still thinking about your comment on growth
curious if this is still on your radar
wanted to follow up on the SEO conversation

{{previous_proposed_time_1}} / {{previou.description: The exact time slots proposed previously.
examples: Thursday at 11 AM
Friday at Noon ET
Tuesday at 10 AM
Wednesday at 1 PM PST

{{sendingAccountName}}.description: Usually sender first name like Rosis.

unsubscribe.action: Do not send. Save log only.

negative_not_interested.action: Do not send unless a polite acknowledgement is clearly required.

call_agenda_examples: how {{companyName}} currently generates and handles qualified opportunities
where ideal prospects may be visiting your website or landing pages without converting
how we identify those missed visitors and route them into personalized cold email campaigns
how we source cold ICP lists and run targeted outbound campaigns in parallel
what messaging angles are most likely to get replies from {{targetBuyerPersona}}
what kind of qualified meeting flow {{companyName}} could realistically expect
how we qualify replies before booking meetings on your calendar
how we protect deliverability while scaling outbound volume
goal_examples: turn existing website traffic into booked sales calls
reduce missed opportunities from visitors who do not fill out forms
create a more consistent pipeline of qualified meetings
get in front of {{targetBuyerPersona}} before competitors do
increase booked calls without adding another SEO or ad effort
make current marketing and sales efforts work harder
build a predictable outbound channel without hiring internal SDRs
friction_removal_examples: to add another SEO or ad effort just to bring in more visitors
to add another tool your team has to manage
to create more manual work for your internal team
to rely on random lead lists
to book meetings with unqualified prospects
to flood your calendar with low-fit calls

core_positioning.what_we_do: Ascendly helps B2B companies generate qualified sales meetings by combining inbound opportunity capture with highly targeted cold email outreach.
core_positioning.how_we_do_it: We identify ideal prospects visiting your website or landing pages, route them into personalized email campaigns, source cold ICP lists, run outbound campaigns, manage replies, qualify interest, and book meetings directly on your calendar.
core_positioning.what_we_do_not_do: We are not adding another SEO, ad, or software tool for the team to manage. We focus on turning existing resources and targeted outbound into qualified sales conversations.
core_positioning.result: The goal is to drive 10-20 qualified sales conversations each month with the kind of customers {{companyName}} actually wants to work with.
